blocks.text: This letter confirms the terms on which Pinebrook Advisory Group LLC will carry out a pricing study for {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}}. I ask that you read it through before you sign. What you need in order to decide is all here: what we will examine, how we will go about it, who will do the work, what it costs, and when we begin. If any of it reads differently than the conversation we have had, I would rather know now than once we are underway.

A pricing study turns on a plain question that is seldom simple to answer: are you being paid what your work is worth. We will look at how your prices are set today and how they hold once they reach a customer, where discounting and terms quietly wear them down, and what it truly costs to serve one account against another. The work rests on your own records, your billings and your cost figures, not on a general theory of what a firm like yours ought to charge. You will not receive a treatise, but a short, ordered set of pricing changes worth making, each one traced to a figure you can check.

Three of our people will carry the engagement. Robert Miller, our Principal Consultant, holds the work and will be your point of contact; he treats a client relationship as his to look after. Jennifer Fletcher, our Senior Consultant, runs the analysis, and she will not let a number reach your desk before it reconciles. Susan Wiley, our Analyst, builds the underlying work the two of them rely on. I read everything before it leaves this office and stand behind all of it.

Our fee for the study is a fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2020-001.fee}}, covering the whole of the work from start through delivery. There are no hourly charges to track and nothing to reconcile at the close. Any out-of-pocket costs would be modest, billed only as incurred and with documentation. We will begin on {{fact:f:E-2020-001.start}} and expect the study to run about eight months from that point.

If this matches your understanding, please sign below and return one copy to me. If it does not, telephone me before you sign; the shape of an engagement is far easier to settle now than in the middle of it. We are glad to have the work, and we mean to give you reason to call on us again.
Kickoff Memo: Pricing Study for Dean PLC
To: Jennifer Fletcher, Susan Wiley. From: Robert Miller. Re: Kickoff for the pricing study, Dean PLC.

The engagement letter is signed and back, and we begin on {{fact:f:E-2020-001.start}}. The job states plainly: tell {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}} whether it is being paid what its work is worth, show where it is not, and say what to do about it. Cynthia Foley, their VP Finance, is our sponsor and our door to the numbers; anything we need from their side goes through her office. We build this from the billings up, from what was actually invoiced and collected, not from anyone's view of what the prices ought to be.

items: Price realization: pull recent invoices and measure what we collected against list, line by line. This is the spine of the study, and everything else hangs off it.
Cost to serve: put a defensible cost against each product line and each major account, so margin is measured rather than assumed.
Discounting and terms: trace where price leaks out after the quote, through discounts, allowances, freight, and payment terms.
Segmentation: group the accounts that behave alike, so any pricing move can be aimed rather than spread flat across the book.

blocks.text: Next steps are short. Susan starts the billing and cost pull this week and gets it into a working file the rest of us can build on. Jennifer sets the framework for cutting margin and cost to serve, so we are all measuring the same way from the first day. I will settle the data request with Cynthia Foley's office and keep her posted on what we need and when. One standing rule: bring me anything that does not tie out the moment you see it. A bad number is cheap to fix now and expensive in front of the client.
Meeting Minutes: Pricing Study for Dean PLC
Working session on the pricing study, held at the offices of {{fact:f:E-2020-001.client}}. Minutes recorded by Susan Wiley and circulated to all present for correction.

items: Robert Miller, Principal Consultant, Pinebrook Advisory Group LLC
Jennifer Fletcher, Senior Consultant, Pinebrook Advisory Group LLC
Susan Wiley, Analyst, Pinebrook Advisory Group LLC
Cynthia Foley, VP Finance, Dean PLC

blocks.text: Robert Miller opened with where the work stands at the midpoint. The billing extract is in hand and reconciled to the general ledger, and a first read of price realization is drafted. The early picture is that realized prices sit well below list on a sizable share of volume, and that the gap runs widest on the smaller accounts. Robert was careful to call these observations and not findings; no recommendation was put forward.

Jennifer Fletcher noted that the cost-to-serve figures are not yet final, and that margin by account cannot be set until they are. She flagged a discrepancy between the freight allowances in the billing file and the figures finance carries, which the team will run down before any account is called unprofitable. Cynthia Foley asked whether payment terms were reflected in the realized-price view; they are not yet, and were added to the open items.

rows: Run down the freight-allowance discrepancy between the billing file and finance's figures
Finalize cost to serve so margin by account can be set
Provide the account-level payment terms for the major customers
Hold the preliminary realization read from the client until the figures reconcile
